parts.title: Part 1 - Multiple-choice cloze
parts.instruction: For questions 1-8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

segments.v: Used coffee grounds ... are already recycled in a (0) variety of ways. Now a team of scientists have discovered that they can also be
to good use as a material for building roads.
Coffee lover and professor ... Arul Arulrajah
throwing away used coffee grounds. He then
the base layer under a road’s
from Melbourne’s coffee houses every week ... This would

questions.text: ... be (1) ____ to good use ...

en.whyCorrect: The fixed collocation is "put something to good use" meaning to use something effectively.

whyWrong.C: "given" shifts meaning to donation; it doesn’t mean 'use effectively' here.

en.keyTakeaway: Learn collocations as chunks: "put something to good use".

vi.whyCorrect: Cụm cố định chuẩn là "put something to good use" = tận dụng hiệu quả.

whyWrong.A: Không có cụm "made to good use".
whyWrong.C: Given làm lệch nghĩa sang cho/tặng, không phải tận dụng.

vi.keyTakeaway: Học collocation theo cụm: "put something to good use".

questions.text: Arul Arulrajah (2) ____ the idea ...
... (3) ____ throwing away ...
He then (4) ____ several experiments ...
... to (5) ____ the base layer ...
... under a road’s (6) ____.
... the team (7) ____ ... every week ...
This would (8) ____ to a reduction ...

parts.title: Part 2 - Open cloze
parts.instruction: For questions 9-16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.

questions.text: ... temperatures ranging (9) ____ 0 to 25 ...

en.whyCorrect: The grammar pattern is "range from X to Y".

vi.whyCorrect: Mẫu chuẩn: "range from X to Y".

questions.text: But (10) ____ is certainly no doubt that ...
Despite (11) ____ located ...
... come (12) ____ a surprise ...
... years (13) ____ average ...
... all (14) ____ a sudden ...
... a (15) ____ short weeks ...
... eager (16) ____ enjoy ...

parts.title: Part 3 - Word formation
parts.instruction: For questions 17-24, use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap.

questions.text: People use it for their (17) ____ too ...
It is found (18) ____ inside rocks ...
... discovered (19) ____ ...
There was such (20) ____ among doctors ...
... prevent a global (21) ____ of the gas ...
... came as a great (22) ____.
... used in the (24) ____ of oil and gas.

parts.title: Part 4 - Key word transformations
parts.instruction: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Use 2-5 words including the word given.

questions.sentence1: Because Alice was finding her book very interesting, she didn’t hear the doorbell ring.
questions.sentence2Template: Alice was __________________________ her book that she didn’t hear the doorbell ring.

questions.sentence1: We missed the train because we didn’t leave home early enough.
questions.sentence2Template: We wouldn’t have missed the train __________________________ home early enough.

questions.sentence1: I’m sure Diane can solve this maths puzzle.
questions.sentence2Template: I’m sure Diane __________________________ this maths puzzle.

questions.sentence1: I’ll lend you my bike, but only if you look after it.
questions.sentence2Template: I’ll lend you my bike __________________________ care of it.

questions.sentence1: ‘I don’t want to help you with your homework, Sam,’ said his sister.
questions.sentence2Template: Sam’s sister said she __________________________ him with his homework.

questions.sentence1: ‘I’m not going to miss my best friend’s party!’ Mary told her mother.
questions.sentence2Template: Mary told her mother that she __________________________ to her best friend’s party.

parts.title: Part 5 - Reading multiple choice
parts.instruction: For questions 31-36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.

questions.text: Harry suggests that David ...

options.A: had thought he would be in a more advanced group.
options.B: was a good role-model for beginners in the group.
options.C: had not listened to the teacher’s instructions.
options.D: was not as skilled as he appeared to believe.

questions.text: What is meant by ‘flailing in the air’?
What does Harry say about his experience of freestyle sports?

options.A: His fear of failure prevented him from participating.
options.B: His natural ability was never fully recognised.
options.C: He discovered that his original opinion of freestylers was justified.
options.D: He regrets not having developed the skills necessary to do well.

questions.text: During Harry’s first attempts at ski jumping, he ...

options.A: felt inhibited by the warning he had been given.
options.B: struggled to overcome his natural instincts.
options.C: showed a determination to improve his technique.
options.D: regarded the fact that he remained upright as progress.

questions.text: While training on the trampoline, Harry ...

options.A: resented the fact that he had to stay indoors.
options.B: wished he had realised how difficult he would find it.
options.C: failed to see the relevance of what he was asked to do.
options.D: felt that the criticism he received was fair.

questions.text: When Harry returned to the slopes after his indoor training, he ...

options.B: became convinced perfect jumps were impossible to achieve.
options.C: got into difficulty after managing a good jump.
options.D: suffered a loss of confidence before his last jump.

parts.title: Part 6 - Gapped text
parts.instruction: Six sentences have been removed. Choose A-G to fit gaps 37-42. One sentence is extra.

options.A: However, many art historians, including Rembrandt experts, were incredibly supportive.
options.B: Once the subject had been determined, the next stage of the process could begin.
options.C: These included details like painting strokes, the artist’s preferred angle and so on.
options.D: As a result, they actually considered giving up on it at this point.
options.E: The team then used technology to add depth and texture to their image.
options.F: The authentic-looking masterpiece is not the work of Rembrandt, though.
options.G: For this reason, the team settled on creating something similar.

questions.text: Gap 37
Gap 38
Gap 39
Gap 40
Gap 41
Gap 42

parts.title: Part 7 - Multiple matching
parts.instruction: For questions 43-52, choose from sections A-D. The sections may be chosen more than once.

blocks.text: How physical exercise makes your brain work better

questions.text: warn that exercising may have the opposite effect to that intended?
mention an increase in the size of something as the result of exercise?
say even very gentle exercise can have a positive impact?
explain why a comparison is a little misleading?

en.keyQuote: Section A: "described ... like a muscle, however, this may not always be a helpful way"
en.whyCorrect: It explicitly says the comparison may be misleading/unhelpful.

whyWrong.B: Discusses endorphins/hippocampus, not an analogy being misleading.
whyWrong.C: Focuses on learning/attention and warnings about extreme workouts.
whyWrong.D: Focuses on gentle exercise and creativity/problem-solving limits.

en.keyTakeaway: In Part 7, match the stem to a unique function (here: challenging an analogy).

vi.keyQuote: Section A: "described ... like a muscle, however, ... not always ... helpful"
vi.whyCorrect: Đoạn A nói thẳng phép so sánh có thể không hữu ích -> dễ gây hiểu sai.

whyWrong.B: Nói về endorphins/hippocampus, không phản biện phép so sánh.
whyWrong.C: Nói về học tập/tập luyện quá sức.
whyWrong.D: Nói về tập nhẹ/creativity và ngoại lệ.

vi.keyTakeaway: Part 7: ghép câu hỏi với chức năng nổi bật của đoạn (ở đây: phản biện so sánh).

questions.text: give an example of an improvement in students’ ability to focus?
say what many people believed about the benefits of exercise has been proved correct?
give advice on how to recall information more effectively?
mention a doubt about why people sometimes experience a change of mood?
warn that a particular type of exercise may not help with problem solving?
welcome the idea of matching exercise type to particular mental challenges?

parts.htmlContent:
Part 1

You must answer this question. Write your answer in 140-190 words in an appropriate style on the separate answer sheet.

1 In your English class you have been talking about physical appearance. Now your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.

Write an essay using all the notes and giving reasons for your point of view.

'We are much too worried about how people and things look.' Do you agree?

Notes

Write about:

  1. 1. being like your friends
  2. 2. the effect of celebrities
  3. 3. ...(your own idea)

Part 2

Write an answer to one of the questions 2-4 in this part. Write your answer in 140-190 words in an appropriate style on the separate answer sheet.

2 You receive this email from your English friend, Sam.

Hi

I'm visiting relatives in Scotland next week. It's a ten-hour train ride! I know that you do quite a bit of long-distance travelling.

What do you take with you on a long journey? And what can I do so I don't get bored?

Thanks

Sam

Write your email.

metadata.audioNote: Set media.url to your GitHub/CDN audio paths.

parts.title: Part 1 - Multiple choice
parts.instruction: You will hear people talking in eight different situations. For questions 1-8, choose the best answer (A, B or C).

transcript.full:
PART 1
Question 1
Boy: What are you reading, Josie?
Girl: An article about some new research. Apparently, some scientists decided to try and find out if fish could recognise different people’s faces.
Boy: That’s a funny topic to choose! Surely fish can’t do that.
Girl: Oh, I dunno. I’ve always felt my goldfish can tell the difference between me and my brother.
Boy: That sounds unlikely. But what did the researchers actually discover?
Girl: Well, they claim to have found that the fish learnt to identify specific faces of the people who fed them. But don’t ask me how they went about proving that - it sounded very complicated. And I’m not entirely sure whether it’s a serious bit of work or not.

Question 2
Girl: It’s your big match next week, isn’t it?
Boy: Yeah - and I’m not looking forward to it. The other team’s really strong. And to be honest, I hate playing in midfield.
Girl: But you did well in your last match.
Boy: I’m better as a striker, though. I might see if the coach would swap me.
Girl: It’s too close to the match now. You could always ask him to fit in more training this week.
Boy: I don’t think there’s any choice, is there? I’ll do that. I was going to suggest that James in our class could play - he’s brilliant - he doesn’t think he can make though.

Question 3
Boy: Have you heard about that band, The Comets? Apparently they’ve split up.
Girl: People are saying online that they all disagreed about which musical direction to take - some want to do heavy rock, others prefer pop stuff.
Boy: They’re just rumours and gossip, if you ask me. There are some strong personalities in the band, though. They were bound to fall out eventually.
Girl: Well, that’s what seems to happen with most bands, isn’t it?
Boy: Just overdoing it might be a factor, you know. They probably burnt themselves out.
Girl: I suppose that is a reason why many bands split. Look at the facts, though - they’ve only done two concerts in the last year.

Question 4
Man: During the class discussion we had today many of you expressed the opinion that space exploration is a huge waste of money. You put forward the argument that we can’t afford it when we have so many problems on Earth that need tackling first. That makes sense on the face of it and you certainly had a good discussion. But of course one of the problems we’re likely to have one day is lack of resources - particularly minerals. On other planets in the solar system we might be able to locate and extract such minerals. Therefore all the money spent now may well turn out to be worthwhile in the long run.

Question 5
Girl: Did you enjoy the boat trip?
Boy: Yeah. It was great to see the city from the river, wasn’t it?
Girl: I’ve done that once before and the buildings are pretty amazing - no more than when you’re looking at them from the bank, though really. What I didn’t get last time was an explanation of what I was looking at - I appreciated that.
Boy: Normally I’m not a fan of commentaries over loud speakers but it added something to the trip.
Girl: And then having music played as we travelled down the river at night made it really cool.
Boy: It was a fantastic evening anyway, so that didn’t really make any difference to me.

Question 6
Boy: Last summer I worked as a volunteer in a nature reserve. I’d often been there when I was younger and was keen to tell everyone what I’d learnt about the place. I didn’t know what to expect, but obviously I knew there’d be given a whole list of options. And I was right. I was given some gardening jobs - not really my thing as I’ve always thought of gardening as pretty dull and for old people. It turned out, though, that I actually managed to make a difference to the place and it was cool just standing back and having a look at what I’d done.

Question 7
[SCRIPT NOT PROVIDED IN THIS DATASET YET]

Question 8
[SCRIPT NOT PROVIDED IN THIS DATASET YET]

questions.text: You hear two students discussing some research into the behaviour of fish. How does the girl feel about the research?

options.A: confused about how the study was conducted
options.B: surprised by the ability the fish displayed
options.C: amused by the subject of the experiment

explanation.en:
Key quote: "don’t ask me how they went about proving that - it sounded very complicated" and "I’m not entirely sure whether it’s a serious bit of work".
Why A: She can’t explain the method and is uncertain, showing confusion.
Why not B: The surprising idea is mentioned, but her final feeling is uncertainty, not amazement.
Why not C: The boy says it’s a funny topic; the girl doesn’t say she feels amused.
Key takeaway: In Part 1, choose the option that matches the speaker’s final attitude words (uncertain/complicated/not sure).
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "đừng hỏi ... chứng minh thế nào - nghe rất phức tạp" và "mình không chắc ... có nghiêm túc không".
Vì sao A đúng: bạn ấy không hiểu cách nghiên cứu được tiến hành và còn phân vân -> cảm giác bối rối/hoang mang.
Vì sao không chọn B: ý tưởng có thể gây ngạc nhiên, nhưng cảm xúc chính của bạn ấy là không chắc chắn.
Vì sao không chọn C: người con trai nói “funny topic”, không phải cảm xúc của cô gái.
Key takeaway: Part 1 - bám vào từ khóa thái độ cuối cùng (not sure/complicated).

questions.text: You hear a boy telling his friend about an important football match he will play in soon. What does he decide to do?

options.A: recommend that another player should join the team
options.B: request further practice sessions before the game
options.C: ask the coach to change his position on the field

explanation.en:
Key quote: Girl: "You could always ask him to fit in more training this week." Boy: "I don’t think there’s any choice, is there? I’ll do that."
Why B: He accepts the suggestion to ask for more training sessions.
Why not A: He mentions James, but says James can’t make it - not a final decision.
Why not C: He considers asking to swap position, but the girl says it’s too close; he drops that plan.
Key takeaway: Track the decision marker ("I’ll do that") and ignore earlier ideas.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: Bạn nữ: "ask him to fit in more training"; bạn nam: "I’ll do that".
Vì sao B đúng: cậu quyết định xin thêm buổi tập.
Vì sao không chọn A: nhắc James chỉ là ý định ban đầu và James không tham gia được.
Vì sao không chọn C: cậu có nghĩ đổi vị trí nhưng bị bác vì quá sát ngày thi đấu.
Key takeaway: nghe câu chốt quyết định (I’ll do that) để chọn đáp án.

questions.text: You hear two friends talking about a rock band. They agree that the band members ...

options.A: had become tired from working too hard
options.C: had very different ideas about music

explanation.en:
Key quote: "There are some strong personalities in the band... They were bound to fall out eventually."
Why B: "bound to fall out" = very likely to argue.
Why not C: Different musical directions are mentioned as rumours; they don’t confirm it as the agreed reason.
Why not A: Burnt out is suggested, but the girl counters with facts (only two concerts), weakening A.
Key takeaway: In agreement questions, pick the line both speakers support without being challenged.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "strong personalities" và "bound to fall out".
Vì sao B đúng: bound to fall out = chắc sẽ cãi nhau.
Vì sao không chọn C: “khác hướng âm nhạc” được nói là tin đồn, không phải kết luận họ cùng thống nhất.
Vì sao không chọn A: “burnt out” chỉ là giả thuyết và bị phản biện (chỉ diễn 2 concert).
Key takeaway: chọn ý được 2 người cùng ủng hộ, không bị phản bác.

questions.text: You hear a teacher talking to his class after a discussion on space exploration. What is he doing?

options.A: challenging his students’ point of view
options.B: asking his students to support their argument
options.C: praising his students for reaching agreement

explanation.en:
Key quote: "That makes sense on the face of it ... But of course ... lack of resources ... Therefore ... worthwhile in the long run."
Why A: He acknowledges their argument then counters it with a different perspective (resources/minerals), i.e., challenges their view.
Why not B: He doesn’t ask for evidence; he provides his own counter-argument.
Why not C: He says they had a good discussion, but the purpose is to change/extend their thinking, not praise agreement.
Key takeaway: "But of course... therefore" often signals the speaker is challenging an earlier opinion.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "makes sense... But of course... Therefore... worthwhile".
Vì sao A đúng: thầy công nhận ý kiến học sinh rồi đưa lập luận phản biện -> đang thách thức/điều chỉnh quan điểm.
Vì sao không chọn B: thầy không yêu cầu học sinh đưa bằng chứng; thầy tự nêu lý do phản biện.
Vì sao không chọn C: có khen thảo luận tốt nhưng mục tiêu là phản biện, không phải khen đạt đồng thuận.
Key takeaway: mẫu "But... therefore" thường là dấu hiệu phản biện.

questions.text: You hear two friends talking about a riverboat trip they’ve been on. What do they both think made it worthwhile?

explanation.en:
Key quote: Girl: "What I didn’t get last time was an explanation ... I appreciated that." Boy: "commentaries ... it added something."
Why A: Both refer to explanation/commentary = information provided.
Why not B: Only the girl likes the music; the boy says it didn’t make a difference.
Why not C: Views are mentioned, but not as the shared reason for being worthwhile; their shared point is the commentary.
Key takeaway: Find the overlap - the same idea said by both speakers.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "explanation" (cô gái) và "commentaries ... added something" (cậu bé).
Vì sao A đúng: cả hai đều đánh giá cao phần thuyết minh/thông tin.
Vì sao không chọn B: chỉ cô gái thấy music “cool”; cậu bé nói không khác biệt.
Vì sao không chọn C: views có nhắc nhưng điểm chung họ cùng đồng ý là phần thuyết minh.
Key takeaway: đáp án đúng là phần giao nhau trong ý của 2 người.

questions.text: You hear a boy talking about working as a volunteer in a nature reserve. What did he feel about the experience?

options.A: surprised by how rewarding he found it
options.B: disappointed that he couldn’t choose what to do
options.C: pleased that he could show how much he knew

explanation.en:
Key quote: "gardening ... pretty dull" -> "It turned out ... I actually managed to make a difference ... it was cool".
Why A: He expected it to be dull but found it rewarding (making a difference).
Why not B: He notes he was given jobs, but the ending is positive and focuses on impact, not disappointment.
Why not C: He mentions he was keen to tell people what he learnt, but the main feeling is satisfaction from results.
Key takeaway: Look for contrast markers like "It turned out" to identify changed feelings.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: nghĩ gardening “dull” nhưng "It turned out ... make a difference ... cool".
Vì sao A đúng: bất ngờ vì trải nghiệm hóa ra rất đáng giá.
Vì sao không chọn B: dù không chọn được việc, cảm xúc kết là tích cực (tạo ra khác biệt), không phải thất vọng.
Vì sao không chọn C: có nhắc muốn chia sẻ hiểu biết, nhưng trọng tâm là hài lòng vì kết quả công việc.
Key takeaway: "It turned out" thường báo hiệu cảm xúc thay đổi.

questions.text: You hear a radio news item about National Ice Cream Month in the USA. What is one ice cream company doing to mark the event?

options.A: designing stamps with illustrations of ice cream on them
options.B: manufacturing a range of ice cream with fewer calories
options.C: introducing some different varieties of ice cream

explanation.en: TODO: Script for Question 7 is not included yet, so key-quote and option elimination cannot be generated reliably without guessing.
explanation.vi: TODO: Chưa có script của câu 7 trong dataset hiện tại nên không thể trích dẫn key quote và loại đáp án một cách chính xác.

questions.text: You hear a girl talking about a science project she did at school. What does she say about the project?

options.A: It helped her with other studies.
options.B: It was interesting to take part in.
options.C: It took too long to set up.

explanation.en: TODO: Script for Question 8 is not included yet, so key-quote and option elimination cannot be generated reliably without guessing.
explanation.vi: TODO: Chưa có script của câu 8 trong dataset hiện tại nên không thể trích dẫn key quote và loại đáp án một cách chính xác.

parts.title: Part 2 - Sentence completion
parts.instruction: You will hear a man called Ben Gardener talking about his job, making large models from plastic building blocks. For questions 9-18, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

transcript.full:
PART 2
Hi! I’m Ben Gardner, here to tell you about my unusual job! ...
... I’d also had a job as an engineer, where I worked in animation ...
... My degree in physics has helped me ...
... One task ... was to produce something round ...
... I decided on a crocodile ...
... And then my drawings - nothing’s more important than those ...
... they do get a lot of handling by visitors ...
... I create the sounds the model will make ...
... a dinosaur was tricky ... tightening screws from the inside ...
... (Other details about models such as whale size, etc.)

questions.text: Ben was an engineer in the field of (9) ______ before getting his current job.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "I’d also had a job as an engineer, where I worked in animation".
Why correct: The speaker names the field directly.
Key takeaway: Part 2 answers are often exact nouns from the script.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "worked in animation".
Vì sao đúng: người nói nêu thẳng lĩnh vực.
Key takeaway: Part 2 thường lấy danh từ đúng nguyên văn từ script.

questions.text: Ben feels his qualification in (10) ______ has been a great help to him in his job.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "My degree in physics has helped me understand ..."
Why correct: He explicitly credits physics.
Trap: Design/art are also mentioned, but not described as ‘his degree’ helping in the same way.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "My degree in physics has helped me...".
Vì sao đúng: nêu trực tiếp bằng cấp giúp ích.
Bẫy: design/art được nhắc nhưng không phải câu chốt về bằng cấp hỗ trợ.

questions.text: At the selection day he attended, Ben had difficulty making something that was (11) ______ in shape.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "One task ... was to produce something round ... It was tough!"
Why correct: round is stated as the difficult shape.
Key takeaway: Listen for contrast like ‘no problem ... one task, though ... tough’.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "produce something round ... It was tough".
Vì sao đúng: round là hình dạng khó.
Key takeaway: nghe đoạn tương phản “no problem ... though ... tough” để bắt ý.

questions.text: To show his range of skills, Ben chose to make a model of a (12) ______.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "I decided on a crocodile ... it got me the job!"
Why correct: the chosen model is named.
Key takeaway: For choice questions, the answer is often the memorable example the speaker highlights.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "I decided on a crocodile".
Vì sao đúng: nói rõ mô hình cậu chọn.
Key takeaway: đáp án thường là ví dụ nổi bật người nói nhấn mạnh.

questions.text: When designing a model, Ben’s (13) ______ are more helpful to him than anything else.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "And then my drawings - nothing’s more important than those".
Why correct: he states drawings are the basis.
Key takeaway: Superlatives like ‘nothing’s more important’ signal the answer.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "my drawings - nothing’s more important".
Vì sao đúng: nhấn mạnh drawings quan trọng nhất.
Key takeaway: cụm nhấn mạnh/superlative thường chốt đáp án.

questions.text: Ben’s models are glued together to prevent them from being broken when (14) ______ touch them.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "they do get a lot of handling by visitors".
Why correct: visitors are the ones handling the models.
Key takeaway: The noun after ‘by’ often names the actor.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "handling by visitors".
Vì sao đúng: visitors là người chạm/đụng vào.
Key takeaway: sau ‘by’ thường là chủ thể thực hiện hành động.

questions.text: Ben particularly enjoys working on the (15) ______ that will accompany a moving model.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "I create the sounds the model will make - that’s the most thrilling bit for me".
Why correct: he says sounds are the most enjoyable part.
Key takeaway: If the speaker says ‘most thrilling’, it usually answers ‘particularly enjoys’.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "create the sounds ... most thrilling".
Vì sao đúng: sounds là phần cậu thích nhất.
Key takeaway: ‘most thrilling’ thường tương ứng với ‘particularly enjoys’.

questions.text: Ben recalls that he had to climb into a huge model of a (16) ______ to make it secure.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "another of a dinosaur was tricky ... tightening screws from the inside".
Why correct: he worked inside the dinosaur model.
Key takeaway: Listen for ‘from the inside’ to match ‘climb into’.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "a dinosaur ... from the inside".
Vì sao đúng: phải xử lý bên trong mô hình dinosaur.
Key takeaway: cụm ‘from the inside’ khớp với ‘climb into’.

questions.text: Ben’s next job will be to construct a model of a (17) ______ from bricks.

explanation.en: TODO: The exact ‘next job’ line is not fully included in the truncated Part 2 transcript above. Confirm by adding the full Part 2 script to generate a quote-based explanation.
explanation.vi: TODO: Trong transcript Part 2 hiện tại bị cắt nên thiếu câu nói về ‘next job’. Cần bổ sung full script Part 2 để trích dẫn và giải thích chuẩn.

questions.text: Before making a model of a building, Ben has to visit it to get the correct (18) ______ of the place.

explanation.en: TODO: The exact ‘visit it to get the correct ...’ line is not fully included in the truncated Part 2 transcript above. Add the full Part 2 script to quote and eliminate alternatives precisely.
explanation.vi: TODO: Transcript Part 2 hiện bị cắt nên thiếu câu chứa ‘visit it ... correct ...’. Cần bổ sung full script Part 2 để trích dẫn chính xác và loại đáp án.

parts.title: Part 3 - Multiple matching
parts.instruction: You will hear five short extracts. For questions 19-23, choose from the list (A-H) what each speaker felt. Use letters only once. Three letters are extra.

options.A: discouraged by how good other people were
options.B: proud of having done detailed research
options.C: disappointed to have felt so nervous
options.D: relieved not to have made any mistakes
options.E: embarrassed at having to change the plan at the last moment
options.F: grateful to have been given support
options.G: annoyed that their efforts were not appreciated
options.H: surprised that practising didn’t help much

transcript.full:
PART 3
Speaker 1: ... class presentations ... classmate did virtually the same ... no choice but to talk about something else ... felt uncomfortable ...
Speaker 2: ... debating ... sounded nervous ... glad I got through it without slipping up ... could’ve done more thorough research ... school won ...
Speaker 3: ... prize day ... nervous ... didn’t take advice ... endlessly rehearsing ... nobody commented ... teacher after me was brilliant ...
Speaker 4: ... interviewed live on TV ... panic ... wasn’t able to calm down ... cross with myself ...
Speaker 5: ... class discussion ... tried without notes ... difficult to avoid getting things wrong ... achievement to have covered as much as I did ...

questions.text: Speaker 1

explanation.en:
Key quote: "one of my classmates did virtually the same ... Now I had no choice but to talk about something else".
Why E: He felt embarrassed/uncomfortable because he had to change plan at the last moment.
Why others: No focus on being proud/relieved; the central emotion is forced last-minute change.
Key takeaway: Match the ‘problem + emotion’ pair (last-minute change -> embarrassed).
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "classmate did virtually the same ... no choice ... talk about something else".
Vì sao E đúng: phải đổi kế hoạch phút chót và cảm thấy không thoải mái/xấu hổ.
Loại đáp án khác: không phải proud/relieved; trọng tâm là đổi kế hoạch đột ngột.
Key takeaway: ghép ‘tình huống + cảm xúc’ (đổi phút chót -> embarrassed).

questions.text: Speaker 2

explanation.en:
Key quote: "glad to say I got through it without slipping up or forgetting key facts".
Why D: He is relieved he made no mistakes.
Why not B: He says he could’ve done more research, so not proud of research.
Key takeaway: ‘without slipping up’ maps directly to ‘no mistakes’.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "got through it without slipping up".
Vì sao D đúng: nhẹ nhõm vì không mắc lỗi.
Vì sao không chọn B: cậu nói lẽ ra cần research kỹ hơn -> không phải tự hào.
Key takeaway: slipping up = mắc lỗi.

questions.text: Speaker 3

explanation.en:
Key quote: "I was too busy endlessly rehearsing ... I just thought that repeating it would pay off ... Nobody commented on it at all".
Why H: He is surprised that practising didn’t help (rehearsed a lot, result still average/ignored).
Key takeaway: Heavy practice + weak impact -> ‘surprised practising didn’t help’.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: luyện “endlessly rehearsing” nhưng kết quả không nổi bật.
Vì sao H đúng: ngạc nhiên vì tập nhiều mà không hiệu quả.
Key takeaway: tập nhiều nhưng tác động nhỏ -> H.

questions.text: Speaker 4

explanation.en:
Key quote: "I was just in a panic and wasn’t able to calm down ... cross with myself".
Why C: Disappointed to have felt so nervous.
Key takeaway: panic/couldn’t calm down -> nervousness regret.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "in a panic ... wasn’t able to calm down".
Vì sao C đúng: thất vọng vì mình quá nervous.
Key takeaway: panic/không bình tĩnh -> C.

questions.text: Speaker 5

explanation.en:
Key quote: "still thought it was an achievement to have covered as much as I did".
Why B: Proud of doing detailed research/covering a lot.
Key takeaway: ‘achievement to have covered as much’ -> pride in preparation/research.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "achievement to have covered as much".
Vì sao B đúng: tự hào vì chuẩn bị/nghiên cứu khá nhiều.
Key takeaway: ‘covered as much’ -> pride in research.

parts.title: Part 4 - Multiple choice
parts.instruction: You will hear an interview with a student called Laura Benson. For questions 24-30, choose the best answer (A, B or C).

transcript.full:
PART 4 (Interview)
Laura: ... Scotland forests peaceful whereas Costa Rica totally the opposite - full of insects, parrots ... all calling out ...
Laura: ... scientists spot half-eaten leaves ... caterpillars would be there ... I had to crawl around searching ... scoop tool to avoid damaging them ...
Laura: ... Mexican biologist Ana ... push them with a stick ... find out self-protection ... drop off leaf ...
Laura: ... pictures in different positions - curled up, stretched out, crawling, munching ...
Laura: ... researchers still don’t know much ... more crucial: caterpillars are food for birds and bats ...
Laura: ... storm, muddy, poisonous snakes ... mosquitoes ... fell in a stream ... wanted to give up ...
Laura: ... changed me ... biology degree would suit me ...

questions.text: What was the first thing that surprised Laura about the rainforest?

explanation.en:
Key quote: "forests are peaceful ... whereas in Costa Rica they’re totally the opposite - full of insects, parrots and other wildlife all calling out".
Why B: She highlights noise (animals calling out) as what struck her first.
Why not A: She says it was hot and humid but she expected tropical weather.
Why not C: She mentions shades of green, but the ‘really struck me’ point is the noise.
Key takeaway: Focus on the phrase ‘That’s what really struck me’.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "totally the opposite - full of ... calling out ... That’s what really struck me".
Vì sao B đúng: điểm gây bất ngờ đầu tiên là sự ồn ào.
Vì sao không chọn A: nóng/ẩm là điều cô đã biết Costa Rica nhiệt đới.
Vì sao không chọn C: có nói xanh nhiều sắc độ, nhưng ‘struck me’ là tiếng ồn.
Key takeaway: bám cụm ‘really struck me’.

questions.text: Why was the task of collecting caterpillars difficult for Laura?

options.A: The idea of handling them made her feel nervous.
options.B: Their appearance made them hard to see.
options.C: She was unsure where to look for them.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "The scientists knew exactly where to find them ... I didn’t have those skills though. I had to crawl around searching".
Why C: She lacked the skills/knowledge of where to look.
Why not A: She says the tool was to avoid damaging them, not fear.
Why not B: Camouflage is mentioned generally, but the difficulty she states is not knowing where to find them.
Key takeaway: Pick the reason explicitly stated as her problem ("I didn’t have those skills").
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "scientists knew exactly where ... I didn’t have those skills".
Vì sao C đúng: cô không biết tìm ở đâu.
Vì sao không chọn A: dụng cụ để tránh làm hỏng chứ không phải sợ.
Vì sao không chọn B: có nói giống lá, nhưng lý do cô nêu là thiếu kỹ năng tìm.
Key takeaway: chọn lý do được nói thẳng (I didn’t have those skills).

questions.text: A scientist from Mexico that Laura worked with was studying ...

options.C: how caterpillars adapt to their environment.

explanation.en:
Key quote: "The idea was to find out what methods of self-protection they have".
Why B: self-protection = defending themselves.
Why not A/C: Not about digestion or adaptation; the target is protection behaviour.
Key takeaway: self-protection is a direct synonym clue.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "methods of self-protection".
Vì sao B đúng: self-protection = tự vệ.
Loại A/C: không nói tiêu hóa hay thích nghi; nói về cơ chế tự vệ.
Key takeaway: bắt synonym trực tiếp self-protection -> defend.

questions.text: What did Laura enjoy taking photographs of?

options.C: the process of caterpillars becoming butterflies

explanation.en:
Key quote: "pictures ... in different positions - curled up, stretched out, crawling ...".
Why A: different positions/shapes.
Why not B: The species were rare/seen before in Colombia, not ‘unknown’.
Why not C: They wanted pictures of caterpillars, not the transformation process.
Key takeaway: ‘different positions’ maps to ‘different shapes/poses’.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "different positions - curled up, stretched out...".
Vì sao A đúng: các tư thế/hình dạng khác nhau.
Vì sao không chọn B: loài hiếm nhưng không phải ‘unknown’.
Vì sao không chọn C: chụp caterpillar ở nhiều tư thế, không phải quá trình biến đổi.
Key takeaway: positions/poses -> shapes.

questions.text: What does Laura think is the most important thing she learned about caterpillars?

options.A: how much damage they can cause to crops
options.B: how little is known about them
options.C: how significant they are for other wildlife

explanation.en:
Key quote: "What’s even more crucial, though, is that caterpillars are food for other animals like birds and bats.".
Why C: She labels this point as ‘more crucial’.
Why not A: Crop damage is mentioned, but not ‘most important’.
Why not B: Lack of knowledge is mentioned, but she elevates the food-chain role as crucial.
Key takeaway: Listen for importance markers: most crucial / most important / the main thing.
explanation.vi:
Câu khóa: "even more crucial ... food for other animals".
Vì sao C đúng: cô nhấn mạnh vai trò chuỗi thức ăn là quan trọng nhất.
Loại A: có nói phá hoại cây trồng nhưng không phải ý ‘crucial’.
Loại B: có nói chưa biết nhiều nhưng ý ‘crucial’ là vai trò với wildlife.
Key takeaway: nghe từ khóa nhấn mạnh mức độ (most crucial).
